scope: FQ-13P2: deliberate generated UI placeholder art for the fifteen reserved hook ids, the first live consumption (hotbar slot frames), and UI-aware auditing. No HUD redesign, no gameplay change.
summary: The FQ-13P0 audit reserved fifteen UI hook ids but had no art for them; FQ-13P2 authors them as deliberate placeholders and wires the first one. scripts/gen_ui_placeholders.py draws fifteen recognizable 32x32 RGBA glyphs into art/generated/ui/ - framed inventory slots (normal, gold-selected, red-invalid), health and attunement orb rings plus a circular fill mask, a dock backplate, six navigation buttons (backpack, helmet, town-hall silhouette, branching skill star, goals scroll, settings gear), and valid/invalid drag cursors - all sharing one palette and a 1px border language, nearest-friendly and deterministic so the generator is idempotent and the PNGs are committed. They are genuine function glyphs, not the meaningless colored squares the spec forbids (a scaled contact sheet was visually reviewed before commit). The HUD now consumes the first surface: the hotbar slots frame with slot_inventory and slot_inventory_selected through _make_slot_style, which returns a StyleBoxTexture when the placeholder exists and the original StyleBoxFlat otherwise, so missing art is never a broken box. The audit tool became UI-aware - the two consumed slots report LIVE, the remaining thirteen report PLACEHOLDER_AUTHORED (reserved for the HUD redesign rather than flagged as orphans), and the reserved-hook report distinguishes authored from still-missing - leaving zero findings and zero data bugs with every UI asset validated at 32x32. Three smoke checks confirm the placeholders load through the ui convention, the live slot actually holds a StyleBoxTexture, and a nonexistent ui id degrades cleanly to null and the flat fallback. Suite 288 -> 291. Consuming the orb, nav-button, dock, and cursor placeholders waits for the HUD redesign, since the current HUD uses bars rather than orbs and has no navigation dock.
acceptance: Fifteen reserved UI ids authored as deliberate 32x32 placeholders with a shared palette + border language (gen_ui_placeholders.py)
Placeholders load through the ui category convention (fq13p2_ui_placeholders_present)
The live hotbar slot consumes the placeholder frame as a StyleBoxTexture (fq13p2_slot_frame_consumed)
A missing ui id is never an error - visual_texture null and StyleBoxFlat fallback (fq13p2_missing_ui_falls_back)
Audit UI-aware: slots LIVE, rest PLACEHOLDER_AUTHORED, 0 findings/0 data bugs, all UI 32x32
Suite 291/291; validator and capsule doctor green
validation: python scripts/asset_audit.py --strict -> exit 0 (0 findings, 0 data bugs)
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited headless run -> 291/291 PASS, 3 new fq13p2_* checks green
git diff --check -> 0 whitespace errors (LF->CRLF notices only)
decisions: Generate deliberate placeholders with PIL (available) as committed PNGs rather than code-drawing them, so they follow one shared palette/border language and are replaceable one file at a time
Consume only the surface that already exists (hotbar slot frames); orb/nav/dock/cursor placeholders are authored + reserved for the HUD redesign rather than forcing a redesign now
_make_slot_style returns StyleBoxTexture when the placeholder exists and the original StyleBoxFlat otherwise, so missing art causes no visual regression
asset_audit.py gains a PLACEHOLDER_AUTHORED state and a UI_CONSUMED set so authored-but-reserved placeholders are documented, not flagged as orphans
--strict enforces the 32x32 UI dimension so a bad regeneration fails validation
Self-review only (no agent spawned); a scaled contact sheet was visually reviewed to ensure the glyphs read as their function
risks: The orb/nav/dock/cursor placeholders are authored but unconsumed until the HUD redesign; validated and documented, not wired
The slot frame is a 9-sliced 32x32 texture stretched over the 42x46 slot; exact margins are tunable in _make_slot_style
next_action: FQ-13P3: player full-body cosmetic pool (design locked). Then FQ-13P4: opening variant-vs-animation distinction + block/item variation follow-through.
